title: The true cost of Britain’s bad deals
gist: Isabel Hilton argues UK defence and net-zero budgets are both underfunded and being pitted against each other, financed by raiding transport and energy budgets, while procurement disasters like the £5bn Ajax and £102.7bn HS2 waste money both sectors need.

insights.claim: Keir Starmer's Defence Investment Plan budget of £15bn came in only marginally above the £13.5bn that triggered defence minister John Healey's resignation weeks earlier.
insights.speaker: Isabel Hilton
insights.so_what: Signals internal government conflict and a fragile UK defence-spending commitment.
insights.locator: The £15bn budget of Keir Starmer’s Defence Investment Plan

insights.claim: The Defence Investment Plan was reached only through vague 'efficiency' savings and by robbing the budgets of transport and of energy security and net zero, and remains well short of the 3.5% of GDP core defence spending Starmer committed at last year's NATO summit for 2035.
insights.speaker: Isabel Hilton
insights.so_what: Reveals defence funding is cannibalising green and infrastructure budgets—a zero-sum fiscal signal.
insights.locator: And it was only reached through vague promises

insights.claim: Abandoning the 2050 net zero goal would inflict economic harm, inflate the bill for climate-related damage, discourage investment, magnify adaptation costs and fatally harm the growing green jobs sector.
insights.speaker: Isabel Hilton
insights.so_what: Frames net-zero rollback as economically destructive, not a saving—counter to right-wing framing.

insights.claim: Renewable energy at an affordable, stable price is foundational to future prosperity and protects the UK from continuing fossil-fuel shocks, making net zero key to national security.
insights.speaker: Isabel Hilton
insights.so_what: Positions energy transition as a security asset, strengthening the case for renewables investment.
insights.locator: Energy security and the fight against climate change

insights.claim: The UK government may be tempted to buy cheap Chinese renewable equipment, but this would bring serious dependency and security risks, with costlier alternatives perhaps the price of national security.
insights.speaker: Isabel Hilton
insights.so_what: Flags a supply-chain security premium favouring non-Chinese clean-energy suppliers.
insights.locator: In renewable energy, the government may be tempted

insights.claim: The £5bn Ajax programme to replace armoured vehicles delivered unusable machines: noise and vibration made crews sick, guns could not be fired, and they could not be used at night, out of 589 ordered.
insights.speaker: Isabel Hilton
insights.so_what: A poster child for UK defence-procurement risk that any defence supplier exposure must weigh.

insights.claim: HS2 is now scheduled to run its first high-speed train between a London suburb and Birmingham by 2039 at a total predicted cost of up to £102.7bn.
insights.speaker: Isabel Hilton
insights.so_what: Emblematic UK megaproject cost overrun—cautionary for infrastructure capital allocation.

insights.claim: Defence procurement is inherently hard because it requires guessing what will be needed 20 to 30 years ahead, then commissioning affordable British equipment amid short-term funding panics, personnel changes and evolving threats.
insights.speaker: Isabel Hilton
insights.so_what: Explains structural difficulty behind recurring UK defence-project failures.
insights.locator: There may be some truth in that

insights.claim: The Treasury regards the Ministry of Defence as a hopeless money drain, an important obstacle to progress on the Strategic Defence Review's case for investment.
insights.speaker: Isabel Hilton
insights.so_what: Institutional Treasury scepticism is a real headwind to sustained UK defence build-out.
insights.locator: But an important obstacle to progress

insights.claim: The reforms promised after Ajax—fixing weak governance, confused reporting lines, high staff turnover, lack of resources and an ineffective programme board—could apply to many UK public spending projects.
insights.speaker: Isabel Hilton
insights.so_what: Generalises procurement failure into a systemic UK delivery problem affecting all big-ticket spend.
insights.locator: In the wake of the Ajax criticisms
